description: Fix the BrowserToolsCard header switch misalignment with PluginCard. The PluginCard header has a PluginMenu (three-dot button) between the accordion trigger and the enable-all switch, but BrowserToolsCard has no menu, causing its switch to sit further left than PluginCard's switch. Add a spacer matching the PluginMenu footprint so both switches align vertically.

userStories.title: Add spacer in BrowserToolsCard header to align switch with PluginCard
userStories.description: As a user, I want the enable-all switch on the Browser Tools card to be vertically aligned with the enable-all switch on plugin cards so the UI looks consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: The BrowserToolsCard header has a spacer element between the accordion trigger and the switch wrapper that matches the width of the PluginMenu button area in PluginCard
The enable-all switch in BrowserToolsCard is right-aligned at the same horizontal position as the enable-all switch in PluginCard
The spacer does not introduce any visible UI elements — it is purely for layout spacing
Storybook stories for SidePanel still render correctly
userStories.notes:
The misalignment is in platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsCard.tsx. In PluginCard.tsx (line 88-153), the AccordionPrimitive.Header has three children after the trigger: 1. PluginMenu — `className="flex shrink-0 items-center px-1"` containing a `h-6 w-6` button 2. Switch wrapper — `className="flex shrink-0 items-center px-3"` In BrowserToolsCard.tsx (line 85-107), the AccordionPrimitive.Header has only two children after the trigger: 1. Switch wrapper — `className="flex shrink-0 items-center px-3"` The PluginMenu occupies `px-1` (4px left + 4px right) + `w-6` (24px) = 32px total width. The BrowserToolsCard is missing this space. Fix: Add a spacer div in BrowserToolsCard between the trigger (line 97) and the switch wrapper (line 98-106) that matches the PluginMenu footprint: ```tsx {/* Spacer matching PluginMenu width so the switch aligns with PluginCard */}
``` This mirrors the exact layout structure of PluginMenu's container: `className="flex shrink-0 items-center px-1"` with a `h-6 w-6` child. Using `aria-hidden` to mark it as purely decorative. Alternatively, a simpler approach is to just add a fixed-width spacer: `
` — but matching the exact PluginMenu structure is more robust if the menu dimensions ever change. Verify visually in Storybook that the switches align. The SidePanel stories are in platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/SidePanel.stories.tsx — note that these currently have type errors (missing browserTools/setBrowserTools props on SearchResults). Fix these story type errors as part of this story if they haven't been fixed yet.
